---
title: "BIOL 490 Field Ornithology"
page-layout: full
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

::::::::: {.hero-home .py-5 .mb-5}
:::::::: {.container-fluid .px-4 .px-lg-5}
::::::: {.row .align-items-center .g-4}
::::: col-lg-6
# Always wanted to learn birds? Start here.

::: {.lead .mb-4}
BIOL 490 is a hands-on summer course where you will learn bird
identification, field observation, ecological skills, and outdoor data
collection through daily field experiences. No prior birding or biology
experience is required. Whether you are new to birds or already
interested in nature, this course is designed to help you build
confidence, develop practical skills, and experience the natural world
in a new way.
:::

::: mb-4
[View the syllabus](syllabus/){.btn .btn-primary .me-2} [See the
schedule](schedule/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .me-2} [Browse course
materials](resources/){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

## Why take this course?

-   Beginner-friendly and open to all majors
-   Learn to identify birds by sight, sound, behavior, and habitat
-   Build outdoor confidence through guided field experiences
-   Gain hands-on experience with surveys, notebooks, and field tools
-   Use binoculars, GPS, eBird, and other real-world tools
-   Explore careers and opportunities in wildlife, ecology, and
    conservation
:::::

::: col-lg-6
![](images/NMU-Students-Birding-Skye-Haas-2017_07_12-18_54_52-UTC (1).jpg){.img-fluid
.rounded .shadow-lg}
:::
:::::::
::::::::
:::::::::

:::::::::: {.container-fluid .px-4 .px-lg-5 .mb-5}
::::::::: {.row .g-4}
:::: col-md-4
::: {.border .rounded-4 .p-4 .h-100}
## What you will do

-   Learn bird identification in real field settings
-   Practice observation, surveys, and data collection outdoors
-   Use binoculars, field guides, apps, and mapping tools
-   Explore local habitats and observe birds where they live
:::
::::

:::: col-md-4
::: {.border .rounded-4 .p-4 .h-100}
## What you will learn

-   How to identify birds by sight, sound, behavior, and habitat
-   Core concepts in bird ecology, migration, and conservation
-   How field biologists record and interpret observations
-   Practical outdoor and scientific skills you can use beyond the
    courses
:::
::::

:::: col-md-4
::: {.border .rounded-4 .p-4 .h-100}
## Who this course is for

-   Students interested in wildlife, ecology, conservation, or field
    biology
-   Students considering environmental careers or graduate study
-   Students who enjoy nature and want to know birds better
-   Beginners with curiosity as well as experienced birders seeking
    growth
:::
::::
:::::::::
::::::::::

::::::::::: {.container-fluid .px-4 .px-lg-5 .mb-5}
:::::::::: {.row .align-items-center .g-4}
::: col-lg-5
## A course built around active learning

This course is built around learning by doing. Students develop skills
through guided field experiences, hands-on practice, structured
activities, and direct observation of birds in real habitats. Each day
emphasizes active participation, skill growth, and learning through
experience.

[Meet the instructor](instructor.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
:::

:::::::: col-lg-7
::::::: {.row .g-3}
::: col-md-6
![Students collecting data
outdoors.](images/Screenshot 2026-04-24 084546.png){.img-fluid .rounded
.shadow-sm}
:::

::: col-md-6
![Bird specimens in a vertebrate museum
collection.](https://heartoflouisiana.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/LSU_Birds_Tray_Tropical_Birds_01_1700x1130-1700x660.jpg){.img-fluid
.rounded .shadow-sm}
:::

::: col-md-6
![Students participating in collaborative course
activities.](images/2.jpg){.img-fluid .rounded .shadow-sm}
:::

::: col-md-6
![Ruddy Duck, one of many species we will
see.](https://naturecanada.ca/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/maleruddyduck-1024x664.jpg){.img-fluid
.rounded .shadow-sm}
:::
:::::::
::::::::
::::::::::
:::::::::::

::: {.container-fluid .px-4 .px-lg-5 .py-4 .mb-4 .bg-light .rounded-4}
## Ready to learn more?

Start with the syllabus, browse the schedule, or look through recent
course materials to see what students actually do in this course.

[Open the syllabus](syllabus/){.btn .btn-primary .me-2} [Open the
schedule](schedule/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .me-2} [View course
resources](resources/){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}

## Thinking about registering?

Register today! The course is filling up so save your spot now. If it's
already full, join the waitlist and more spots may be opened.

Not sure if the course is for you? Register now and decide later!
:::
